one_line_positioning: NIFT-trained merchandiser who scaled a fashion category to 25+ subcategories and achieved 35% product margins via direct vendor negotiation — equipped to drive profitability in H&M's women's jersey segment
narrative_angle: A NIFT graduate who both launched a full fashion category from near-zero and managed supplier delivery across 20+ partners — bringing entrepreneurial P&L ownership and proven margin improvement. While new to SAP, my WIP tracking and inventory optimization experience ensures rapid adoption of H&M's systems.
lead_strengths: Expanded fashion category from 5 to 25+ subcategories, generating ₹3.5 lakh revenue in two months
Improved product margins from 22% to 35% through vendor negotiations
Managed PO/PI across 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories with 95% on-time delivery
proof_points_to_emphasize: Expanded from 5 to 25+ subcategories at SuperK
Managed 20+ suppliers and 10+ factories at Go Colors, achieving 95% on-time delivery
Improved margins from 22% to 35% at SuperK
Launched women's western-wear brand focusing on tops, skirts, dresses
things_to_downplay: Explicit OTB management experience (candidate has not handled formal open-to-buy budgets in a large retail structure)
SAP/ERP system proficiency (no direct experience with SAP, may require ramp-up)
Omnichannel merchandising experience is limited to 6 months across online and 3 offline stores
tone_guidance: Lead with metrics (%, ₹, on-time delivery). Use retail-specific terminology (sell-through, MOQs, buy plans, range planning). Emphasize P&L ownership and supplier outcomes. Avoid generic enthusiasm about fashion.
recruiter_first_impression_target: This candidate has P&L ownership and tangible results in scaling a category, not just buying execution — a high-potential hire who may need ramp-up on corporate ERP systems.
reasoning: Candidate demonstrates strong core merchandising competencies with direct evidence of assortment planning, vendor negotiation, and PO/PI management, aligning well with the role. However, the absence of OTB management and SAP experience are significant gaps for a senior position at H&M, making this a careful recommendation that should highlight transferable inventory management skills and fast learning to mitigate risk.
